title: voiage: Value of Information for Research, Implementation, and Decision Making

authors.name: Dylan A. Mordaunt
authors.affiliation: Faculty of Health, Education and Psychology, Victoria University of Wellington; College of Medicine and Public Health, Flinders University; Centre for Health Policy, The University of Melbourne

abstract: Parameter-focused Value of Information analysis does not by itself represent uncertainty about model structure, population differences, viewpoint, implementation, evidence quality, or the timing and reversibility of action. voiage provides a common way to describe and analyse these wider decision problems. It keeps choices, groups, populations, assumptions, warnings, and data sources visible while distinguishing established calculations from newer features that still need applied validation.
